body_txt: A cat chasing a car? They've seen it. People playing card games for the fate of the world ? They've seen it. They've explained the Noodle Incident twice . They even know what the Cow Tools are for. Yes, these characters have Seen It All . The way the trope's portrayed varies depending on the characters. They may either become The Stoic since they've seen everything they have, or they may simply be Deadpan Snarkers . It may be they've developed odd coping mechanisms, like a Doctors Teasing Patients . Maybe they've seen so much that they make sure they're ready for anything . They may or may not also be smug about the fact that they've seen it all already. These portrayals, however, while very common, aren't the only ways to portray this trope. Compare Dull Surprise , Fantastically Indifferent , Oh, No... Not Again! , Prescience Is Predictable , The Anticipator , Memorized the Whole Process , and "Clichéd Villain" Accusation . See also Seen-It-All Suicide and Nothing Left to Do but Die . Not to be confused with Wake Up, Go to School, Save the World . Beings that are Really Seven Hundred Years Old or from the Time Abyss have a high chance of having seen it all already. Often happens to a Weirdness Magnet . Contrast I've Never Seen Anything Like This Before and Didn't See That Coming . Sister trope to Conditioned to Accept Horror . No relation to Seen-It-All Suicide , where characters off themselves after witnessing some truly bizarre sight.
